data.title: Nvidia: Poised To Break $200 After Q2 Earnings
data.description:
Introduction

My previous optimistic Nvidia Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) quarterly earnings preview was good because the stock is currently 33% more expensive that it was on May 23. The Q2 release is getting closer (August 27th), and I remain optimistic before it as well. Reasons for bullishness include accelerating AI tailwinds as well as Nvidia's dominance in the industry. Moreover, Nvidia is a real earnings surprise champion that very rarely misses consensus forecasts. The pullback of the last few days looks like a prime opportunity to buy NVDA before earnings. NVDA is still a Strong Buy.

NVDA Q2 earnings preview

The company is expected to publish its Q2 earnings on August 27th, during post-market hours. Nvidia's status of the largest company in the U.S. (by market cap) means that their earnings release will be important for the entire stock market. Analysts from Wedbush are confident that Nvidia is set to deliver another blowout earnings that will make happy the broader market as well.

From my perspective, the fact that Nvidia never missed consensus forecasts over the last ten quarters is obviously good news for the broader market. Moreover, Nvidia's EPS beats are usually quite wide.

Wall Street analysts expect Nvidia to deliver a 53% year-over-year revenue growth in Q2. Forecast for the company's EPS is also positive with the expected 48% year-over-year increase for the bottom line. On the other hand, the Wall Street sentiment around Nvidia's upcoming earnings release is slightly more bearish since down revisions prevailed over upgrades over the last 90 days. This is a two-edged sword though, because beating conservative forecasts will be easier for NVDA.

The company's Data Center segment accounts for around 90% of its total sales, being still the primary engine for growth. I am highly confident in this segment because it has massive AI tailwinds behind its back. The Q2 earnings season of cloud giants (Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet/Google (GOOGL)) revealed that they remain extremely aggressive in their plans to expand their data center infrastructures.

For example, Google increased its FY2025 AI capex guidance quite aggressively, from $75 billion to $85 billion. According to recent information from TechRadar, Amazon's annual capital expenditure for data centers already topped staggering $100 billion. Microsoft is also quite aggressive, as the company announced expansion of its AI infrastructure to the heart of Europe, Austria, in late July. With all these firmly bullish developments around Nvidia, I expect an upbeat FY2026 guidance from the management as well.

Another positive factor that is likely to boost Nvidia's FY2026 guidance upgrade potential is much more certainty regarding the ability to export chips to China. I do not think that a 15% charge that will be paid from chip sales to China will be a big problem for Nvidia, taking into account its massive pricing power due to unmatched performance of its products. Therefore, I am confident that the effect of this 15% charge will be mostly passed on to Chinese customers of Nvidia and will not put pressure on margins demonstrated at the moment.

However, Nvidia's earnings are never solely about numbers and financials. CEO Jensen Huang is an exceptional visionary, and what he says about the potential of AI is highly likely to be another source of optimism for investors. Nvidia has been historically the company that opens new markets and opportunities from scratch thanks to its unparalleled commitment to innovate. If we look at the company's R&D spending line chart, the innovation budget is soaring exponentially. With such an aggressive reinvestment in innovation, I am sure that the management will unveil plans about new groundbreaking products and technologies that will further fortify Nvidia's AI ecosystem.

With its market cap of above $4 trillion and just $10 billion in debt, Nvidia has almost unlimited potential to exercise financial flexibility. Raising more funds either through debt or equity looks like no problem for Nvidia. This is another crucial factor that is highly likely to keep the management confident in their ability to ramp up R&D or M&A extremely aggressively. With Nvidia's absolutely phenomenal ROIC of around 100%, I am extremely confident that the vast majority of ventures and growth initiatives will be valuable for investors.

Therefore, I think that everything speaks bullish before Nvidia's upcoming earnings release. It is no surprise why did several prominent banks raised their price targets ahead of earnings.

Moreover, the market does not seem very greedy about Nvidia ahead of earnings, which is also good for long investors. The 14-day RSI of around 50 indicates neutral sentiment, which means that there is not much pressure from potentially unrealistic expectations.

Valuation analysis

Nvidia's estimated forward P/E ratio trajectory until FY2029 looks extremely bullish. The company's P/E is expected to compress significantly by FY2029, even considering the expected aggressive EPS growth deceleration. For the dominant force in the AI revolution this forward P/E ratio setup is no doubt bullish, signaling significant undervaluation.

The estimated FY2026 forward non-GAAP P/E ratio of 40 is significantly lower than Nvidia's historical average of almost 47. The average opinion of 47 Wall Street analysts says that this fiscal year's (FY2026) EPS will be $4.35.

Strong AI tailwinds and Nvidia's dominance in processors for data centers provide Wall Street analysts with great visibility into the company's future performance, meaning that the $4.35 EPS forecast is highly reliable.

If we multiply the projected annual EPS of $4.35 by historical forward non-GAAP P/E ratio of 46.88, we get the fair share price of approximately $204. With that being said, NVDA is undervalued by 17%, which is a compelling discount for the world's largest AI beneficiary.

Mitigating factors

Even for the world's largest company and absolute stock market superstar like Nvidia, there is no guarantee that they will deliver blowout earnings. Despite AI tailwinds being strong and will highly likely support massive Data Center revenue growth, there might be some temporary setbacks. For example, some temporary supply chain issues might be the reason for Q2 revenue performance below expectations.

Lower-than-expected revenue in case of temporary setbacks will not be a big fundamental problem, but the stock market is a voting machine over short term periods. Therefore, the share price might suffer in this case. Moreover, Wall Street analysts see quite limited upside potential with an average target price of $191.5. A relatively modest upside suggested by Wall Street might be another headwind for the share price after earnings.

Conclusion

I am extremely optimistic about the upcoming earnings release by Nvidia. The company has a perfect earnings surprise record over more than two years, which means that the management is highly committed to delivering on their financial plans and promises. There is no hype around the stock before Q2 earnings and the valuation looks extremely attractive, meaning that NVDA is still a no-brainer Strong Buy.

data.title: Youdao: Still A Buy On Earnings Breakthrough And Potential Buyback Resumption
data.description:
I'm staying bullish on Youdao, Inc. (NYSE:DAO). Its above-consensus 2Q25 results and encouraging buybacks guidance suggest that the shares are worthy of a Buy.

My previous August 24, 2024 article touched on DAO's reduced losses for 2Q24 and the high likelihood of a further turnaround.

Maiden Q2 Profit

Its key financial metrics for the recent three-month period revealed last week surpassed expectations.

Topline for DAO rose +7 percent year-on-year to CNY 1,418M in 2Q2025. This was way better than its sales contraction of -10% and -7% for 4Q24 and 1Q25, respectively.

The firm's latest quarterly revenues also came in 3% above consensus according to S&P Capital IQ data.

The "Online Marketing Services" or "OMS" division was the star in the second quarter.

In its 20-F filing, OMS is described as a provider of "performance-based advertising solutions, where users click on our advertisers’ promotional links displayed on our (online learning) platforms."

OMS's top line jumped +24% YoY to CNY 0.63B for 2Q25, which was a new historical high. "The increased demand from gaming industry and overseas markets" was cited as the reason for outperformance in the group's media release.

NetEase (NTES), a Chinese games giant, has a 9% stake in DAO. It's reasonable that the latter had exploited synergies between the two to generate gaming-related advertising opportunities for OMS.

With regards to growth outside of its home market China, DAO's Q2 analyst briefing revealed that it "signed partnerships with TikTok last year and with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) this year." GOOG-related revenue contribution has more than doubled from the initial month when the alliance first began.

"Learning Services Segment (LSS)" and OMS are DAO's major businesses accounting for 46% and 45% of its 2Q25 sales, respectively. LSS also reported positive sales expansion (+2%) in the quarter, but OMS stole the show this time.

Its LSS-related platforms offer "STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, the Arts and Mathematics) courses and adult (Massive Open Online Course or MOOC) courses" as indicated in the 20-K filing.

DAO's 2Q2025 EBIT of CNY 29M was a substantial +23% beat (source: S&P Capital IQ) compared to consensus. Also, it had suffered from a -CNY 0.07B operating loss in the same period last year.

Its "Sales & Marketing" or "S&M" expenses decreased 22% to CNY 0.40B in 2Q25 on YoY terms. This means that the enterprise's S&M-to-revenue ratio was 10.7ppts (percentage points) lower for the recent quarter.

Since its public listing in October 2019, DAO has been loss-making in every single Q2 until now. It finally registered a 2Q25 normalized bottomline of +CNY 13M, representing a big breakthrough. That was also the fourth profitable quarter in a row starting in 3Q2024.

At the company's results call, DAO noted that it will be "leveraging our AI advantages to enhance operational efficiency." It also emphasized that the group "continues to move quickly in the GenAI-powered ad technology space."

I think that AI-linked tailwinds will allow Youdao to continue growing the OMS unit and keeping S&M costs low.

DAO should be able to meet its FY25 goal of positive "Operating Cash Flow" or "OCF" in my opinion. Its FY2024 OCF was -CNY 70M (vs +0.19B in 2Q25), so a reversal could re-rate its shares.

Favorable Shareholder Return Outlook

DAO guided at the 2Q25 earnings meeting that it "will process the rest of our budget for the share buyback in the next few quarters." The firm also indicated that it might "present a new (repurchase) plan" after the old one is concluded.

Its $0.04B three-year buyback plan is slated to end in November this year.

The company has already completed 85% of the existing program as of June 30, 2025. But it hasn't bought back any of its own shares for one whole year in the 3Q24-2Q25 time frame.

The latest management remarks suggests that DAO will likely execute on the remaining $6.2M of repurchases in the Aug-Nov period this year. Additional buybacks might also be done in Nov-Dec '25, assuming a new capital return proposal is put forward as per the company's comments.

Youdao doesn't pay a dividend, so stock purchases are the sole means of stockholder cash distributions. Also, its valuations are enticing at a forward Price-to-Sales of 1.26x, or 42% below its historical trading average.

A resumption of buybacks will be an critical catalyst. It helps to send the message that DAO's financials have improved, and the shares are cheap.

You Should Consider These Worst-Case Scenarios

A failure to maintain positive bottomline and OCF for subsequent quarters or the full year will leave the market disappointed. This could be because of an increase in investments, or operating de-leverage driven by slower revenue growth.

If DAO doesn't start returning capital again as promised, there will be doubts about its future performance and cash management approach.

My Conclusion

Youdao is still deserving of being a Buy-rated name based on my analysis.

The stock can trade +24% (1.56/1.26) higher, if its P/S re-rates to Chinese online advertising peer Weibo's (WB) current 1.56 times multiple.

Catalysts include the achievement of the full-year OCF target and the kick-start of repurchases again.

data.title: VisionWave Holdings Won't Make It Into Our Holdings Just Yet
data.description:
Introduction

VisionWave Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:VWAV) is a small firm that recently went public through a combination with a blank check company, Bannix Acquisition Corp. The firm is working on developing next-generation defense technologies by integrating AI across their autonomous vehicles solutions group. Their products are optimized for real-time threat detection and operational efficiency. They were founded in September 2024, and we believe that this incredibly early-stage company has an interesting product vision and we hope that they can deliver and execute. The following section provides a review of some recent company news.

Recent News Analysis

Being so early stage, VWAV faces the current priorities of filing for the necessary patents to lock in their product. As mentioned above, the firm’s products take in radio and visual signals from across multiple terrains and domains, runs the data through an AI engine, and provides an output in the form of tactical next steps in defense use cases. There were some follow-up questions from the US Patent and Trademark Office (US PTO), in which the firm recently filed a response with regards to its radio wave finder technology continuation patent that was filed, and they also introduced a new AI engine called Evolved Intelligence. Essentially, the US PTO sought additional clarity on the company’s technology that describes a multiplanar radio wave detection and imaging system, paired with an AI-powered analysis and screening tool that can review data across air, water, ground, and space domains.

Further, the Evolved Intelligence engine that the firm recently developed is designed to combine logic and data-driven models with an added reasoning layer, which helps with the goal of enabling more context-aware decision support for tactical military responses in real time. The company states that there will be fully-autonomous drones and vehicles that will be powered by this AI engine, which, if executed properly, could provide stable split-second decision making and outcome analysis for the end users. We view this as a very promising tool and platform that can only have legs if 1) there is strong execution and 2) potential for government contracting / testing.

The company recently did secure an equity line of funding of $50 million from an institutional investor to give them the capital and time to work on a proof of concept, and the same investor also committed to funding $5 million more through convertible notes. VWAV is able to sell up to $50M in common stock over the next 24 month period, at its discretion, to enable this funding. We expect that through this funding, the firm can develop a genuine proof of concept and take it to market.

CEO Background

With such early-stage firms like this, it is important to learn about the background of leadership. Noam Kenig is the CEO, and he does have some experience in this general industry. From an older article published in 2019, it looks like Kenig was the CEO of Aerial X, a drone company with a core product of a kamikaze style drone that could be used to intercept missiles or roadside bombs. This is along the same lines and certainly the same defense industry innovation that his current company, VWAV, operates in. This gives us some faith in his leadership in that he already has been an operator in this field. According to the website of Aerial X, they were able to get an audience with the US Special Operations Command and the US Strategic Capabilities Office - positive signs that he has credibility in this industry.

Company is operating in the right time frame

As the firm is working to develop drones, there has never been a better time to be invested in this industry. Per the following graph, it is clear that overall military drone spending is steadily increasing.

This forecast, showing 12.2% CAGR through 2030, makes us bullish on the overall future of autonomous drones and we hope that with proper execution, VWAV’s product portfolio can take some share from this market. Further, it is clear that NATO countries overall will be expected to increase their spending on defense - as per the 5% of GDP target that has been established. To see what the nominal figures would look like once this is in place, please see the below graph.

We view this macro backdrop as beneficial for VWAV - as long as they can execute.

Financials

VWAV has very recently gone public so there is limited financial data. We look at the most recent 10-Q which was filed on August 19th to understand the company’s financial position. In the 10-Q, the company has reported results from the quarter ending June 30th and in some cases also has data for the 6 months ending June 30th as well as comparisons of the same time period in the previous year.

Highlights:

  • The company has no revenue as of yet

  • Operating losses were $340.6K for the quarter and $688.0K for the half

    • Operating losses were 2.23% smaller than the same quarter last year and 10.47% lower than the same half last year.

    • Positive take: Good to see a company with $0 revenue is decreasing its operating losses.

    • Negative take: In order to achieve profitability, the company may need to invest more into is operations

  • Net losses were $434.3K for the quarter and $861.8K for the half.

    • Compared to the previous year’s quarter and half the net losses were 183.66% and 375.26% larger respectively

    • The net losses in the previous year were significantly impacted by interest earned on cash in a trust account. If we remove the impacts of that income from both this year and last year's numbers, we see that on a YOY quarterly basis the net loss increased by 24.67% while on a YOY half basis the net loss increased by 19.92%

Overall, it is hard to make any strong assertions on the company’s financials due to lack of data. However we generally are more skeptical of “pre-revenue” companies as investing in them requires a level of faith in the company’s ability to do business.

Risks

No Revenue

The biggest risk we see here is that the company has not earned any revenue selling products. The only income we see so far is from interest on cash that the company holds in a trust (that it has since divested out of). In our eyes, a $0 revenue company is a lot riskier than a company making only $0.50 in revenue. A $0 revenue company has no proven product-market fit and no evidence that their offerings are viable in the market.

Funding

In the most recent half, the company has posted a net loss of $861.8K which is $1,723.6K on an annualized basis. The company only has $885 in cash as of the most recent report and $1.2M in a trust account. We see that without any cash infusion, the company is not in a position to continue funding its operations for long. Of course the recent $50M equity line of funding will help but it will involve shareholder dilution and won’t last long if the company ramps up its spending to fuel growth.

Conclusion

To conclude, we view that VWAV operates in an enticing market with lots of potential. If they can execute, there could be large financial rewards for early investors. Currently, however, it remains to be seen how this company will fare. Any investment in this company should be viewed as a “Venture Capital” style of investment - big chance of being unprofitable, but a small possibility of hitting it big. Only long-term investors with significantly high-risk tolerances should look to put a fraction of their portfolio into this company.

data.title: Alibaba: Getting All Ready For The Breakout Higher
data.description:
Alibaba: Bullish Sentiments Have Returned

China's e-commerce behemoth is having a pretty decent run as we close in on the final week before Alibaba Group Holding (NYSE:BABA) post its earnings release for the June quarter. Recently, BABA has benefited from the improved buying sentiments in Chinese equities. Hence, the rising tide has arguably also lifted BABA, although I must highlight that the stock has failed to outperform its KraneShares CSI China Internet ETF peers (KWEB) since topping out earlier this year.

I don't think I have to remind you that the competition between Alibaba and its leading China tech peers have magnified recently. As JD.com (JD) entered the delivery battle royal, contending with Meituan (OTCPK:MPNGF) and BABA, Alibaba has also taken steps to further consolidate its broader e-commerce ecosystem. As a reminder, the company has also reportedly implemented a cross-platform loyalty program, extending the benefits across e-commerce, food delivery, and travel services. Hence, it follows closely on the company's earlier decision to integrate its delivery and travel services platform, leveraging BABA's massive scale to enhance its value proposition for its customers.

Whether the so-called "user-first" strategy to bolster platform engagement and monetization can truly succeed remains to be seen as the battle unfolds. Nevertheless, Chinese authorities seem determined to clamp down on destructive competition (also known as involution or 内卷), but possibly also cognizant about not causing mayhem on the Chinese market again, given how nascent and delicate China's economic recovery has progressed.

Alibaba: Emerging AI Prowess Underpins AliCloud's Growth

In my previous Alibaba writeup, I articulated management is focused on executing well, while also navigating its relatively undervalued stock with accretive share repurchases. Therefore, I believe it has arguably helped provide a sustained floor for BABA in July 2025, aligning with the more optimistic buying sentiments in Chinese equities.

With the stock having outperformed the S&P 500 (SPX) (SPY) since then, should investors who missed the advance take a stab at the current rally and try to catch the next wave up?

Let's be clear here. I think Alibaba largely remains a work in progress in its recovery efforts. The upcoming Banma IPO (smart automobile tech) could demonstrate Alibaba's ability to monetize its sprawling investment portfolio/asset base and rechannel its wherewithal into the emerging race for AI primacy. Note that Alibaba has emerged as one of the forerunners in China's cutthroat, but incredibly innovative AI ecosystem. Not only has Alibaba's execution in the open source area achieved high benchmark scores, the company is focusing on Applied AI to leapfrog over its smaller peers.

Key to the company's growing competencies in AI is its ability to capitalize on the growing prowess of Alibaba Cloud, as it aims to scale up further. Notably Alicloud has improved its adjusted EBITA by more than 70% over the past 12 months, justifying its profitability growth trajectory.

Notably, Alibaba management believes that the current AI race is still too early for us to determine the outright winners now, as AI capabilities continue to evolve rapidly. Nevertheless, I believe BABA's immense scale, solid fundamentals and a still sticky consumer e-commerce ecosystem should afford the company tremendous advantages to scale its AI infra and platform competencies over and above that of its peers.

Alibaba: Profitability Recovery Unlikely Toppled By Recent Price War

Hence, I assess that the recent improvement in BABA's buying cadence is consistent with the possible inflection in its topline growth over the next 12 months. Therefore, I expect management to provide a more sanguine outlook for the next few quarters, which should keep Wall Street firmly in the bullish side of the ledger.

While the delivery price war between the feuding incumbents could intensify the competitive landscape in the near term, I believe Alibaba's ecosystem advantages and platform incumbencies should help maintain its profitability resilience.

Nevertheless, China's consumer spending has been somewhat in a rut as China's property market hasn't quite staged a remarkable turnaround. Hence, while the ascendancy in Chinese equities have lent credence to a more optimistic backdrop, we must still scrutinize whether the follow-through in improved consumer spending sentiments could pan out. And I think that proposition has yet to be fully validated.

And we must also consider the fact that the adjusted EBITA of Alibaba's Taobao and Tmall group remains more than 18x that of its cloud computing unit. Hence, betting on a surge in Alibaba's ability to scale its AI infra while monetizing its platform significantly may not yield meaningful bottom-line growth in the near term in the early stages.

Buy Or Sell BABA Stock?

The good news for investors of Alibaba is that the stock has followed through with constructive price action over the past year. Hence, I believe it corroborates my conviction that BABA's forward EBITDA multiple of 8.7x (about 20% under sector median) still offers a reasonable margin of safety, notwithstanding its recent outperformance.

I believe you can also glean the higher-lows price structures seen in the above BABA chart. Notably, the bottoms in April and July 2025 attracted robust dip-buying support, augmenting the stock's uptrend recovery.

While I note that the stock has been consolidating over the past four to five weeks, I've not observed ominous signs that we must be extremely cautious about. Hence, I urge investors to stay vested, while new buyers may want to use the current opportunity to increase exposure as we head into Alibaba's upcoming earnings scorecard.

Rating: Maintain Buy.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Expansion Into Metro Atlanta Looks Promising For First Community Corporation
data.description:
On July 23, First Community Corporation (NASDAQ: FCCO) released its Q2 2025 quarterly results that included multiple record-breaking marks for the company. Its assets under management crossed the $1 billion mark for the first time. Earnings of $0.68 per share were also in record territory, beating expectations by $0.12 or 21.4%.

There is more to investing in FCCO than just one quarter of results, however. Its stock is up over 23% in the last 12 months when dividends are included. First Community has recently announced a strategic acquisition to expand its geographic footprint, and the company is showing steady improvements in almost all segments of its business. I am rating the stock as a Buy at its current levels, where it trades at 10.4 times current earnings in spite of its strong results from the past year.

Company Overview

Valued by the market at just under $200 million, First Community Corporation is the holding company for First Community Bank, a small regional bank that operates primarily within the state borders of South Carolina. On July 14, the company announced a plan to expand in the Metro Atlanta area with the acquisition of Signature Bank of Georgia (OTCPK: SGBG). The all-stock transaction has a value of $41.6 million based on the price of FCCO at the time. The deal is accretive to First Community's earnings per share by about 4.4% next year. A map of the current First Community Bank locations are shown in the map below. The addition of the Signature Bank branches will be a significant push westward for the company.

According to the company’s most recent 10-Q filing, First Community Bank controls $2.05 billion of total assets, a 4.5% increase from the end of 2024. The deal with Signature Bank should bring that total up to around $2.3 billion. Total deposits for FCCO currently total $1.75 billion, with an impressive $476 million, or 27.2%, being non-interest-bearing. Loans held for investment have a value of $1.26 billion. After the merger, deposits are expected to increase to $2 billion while loans will climb to $1.5 billion.

A breakdown of the current FCCO loan portfolio is shown below. Commercial real estate takes up the largest share by far at $818.7 million or 65.0%. Construction real estate has the second largest at $156 million or 12.4% followed closely by residential mortgages at $128.8 million.

FCCO currently pays a quarterly dividend of $0.16, having raised it from $0.15 in July of this year. This is the fourth straight year that the company has raised its dividend. Since 2012, First Community has increased its annual payout 12 times. The 10-year compound average growth rate for its dividend is 8.5%.

Analysis of First Community Corporation

There were several highlights for Q2 2025 for the company to announce last month in its most recent quarterly update. After its strong earnings report for the most recent three-month period, diluted earnings for the first six months are at $1.18 per share, an increase of over 55% from the first half of 2024. After earning $1.82 in FY 2024, FFCO is expected to report FY 2025 earnings of $2.52 and FY 2026 profit of $2.92. This means the company is expected to grow earnings by 38.5% in FY 2025 and another 15.9% in FY 2026.

First Community benefits from a healthy and growing deposit base. Total deposits for the bank were growing at a 6.6% annualized growth rate at the end of Q2. Pure deposits (total deposits minus certificates of deposit) were growing at an annualized rate of 6.5%. Net interest margin of 3.16% was an improvement of 31 basis points over the same time last year. It should be noted that the bank FCCO is acquiring has a NIM of 4.88%, one of the highest I have seen in my analysis of any bank on Seeking Alpha.

First Community's loan portfolio looks healthy, having grown at an annualized rate of 6.5%. The company reported non-performing assets of 0.02% at the end of Q2. Past due loans and non-accrual loans also equaled 0.02% as of June 30, 2025. FCCO’s Common Equity Tier I ratio was 13.04% at the end of Q2, compared to 12.56% at the same time in 2024. First Community also had a leverage ratio of 8.44%, which is well above the requirements for the bank to be considered well capitalized. However, Signature Bank has even more impressive numbers with a Tier 1 Risk-Based Capital Ratio of just over 16.5% and a leverage ratio of 13.63%.

In the company’s Q2 report, First Community Bank President and CEO Ted Nissen made the following statements with regard to the bank’s loans:

Loan production continued strong in the second quarter, but was significantly offset by much higher loan payoffs during the quarter. We are pleased that our annualized growth rate for loans is still solid at 6.5% and we see positive momentum going into the third quarter.

He added this about the bank's deposits:

A strength of our bank has been and continues to be the value of our deposit franchise. In the second quarter of 2025, we saw growth in total deposits, pure deposits and non-interest-bearing deposits. While customer cash management accounts were down on a linked quarter due to seasonality in those deposits, they were up 74.8% year-over-year. In addition, with this growth, we were still able to continue to proactively manage the pricing of our interest-bearing deposit products and saw a decrease in the cost of deposits and cost of funds during the second quarter of 2025."

First Community has undoubtedly benefited from a strong local economy in South Carolina. During Q1 2025, South Carolina’s real GDP grew at an annualized rate of 1.7%, higher than any other state in the US. These local tailwinds have been important for the bank’s success. The state also ranked fourth in the country during the first three months of this year in personal income growth at an annualized rate of 9.1%.

Peer Analysis

Anytime I am researching a company, I like to see how it compares to its peers, in addition to how well it suits my standards on a stand alone basis. Seeking Alpha identifies five regional banks as peers for FCCO that are all within $10 million of its market cap size. While there are differences in their service areas, these banks all deal with similar issues based on their asset bases. The peers currently identified by Seeking Alpha are as follows:

  • First National Corporation (FXNC)
  • First Internet Bancorp (INBK)
  • First Western Financial (MYFW)
  • OP Bancorp (OPBK)
  • Provident Bancorp (PVBC)

Here is how First Community compares to the averages from this group.

First Community Corporation

Peer Group Average

Price/ Earnings (FY 2025)

10.4

14.2

Price/ Earnings (FY 2026)

9.0

10.11

PEG GAAP (TTM)

0.21

Revenue Growth (YoY)

21.4%

Revenue Growth (FWD)

ROA

1.28%

ROE

Dividend Yield

2.44%

1.62%

For each metric considered here, FCCO is above average, often by a significant amount. There is only one area where the First Community is below average, and that is its price to book ratio of 1.3. However, the bank has been able to increase its tangible book value by over 11% annually over the last three years. When considered along with the other positives mentioned here, I think there is still enough to justify a bullish stance on the stock.

Risks to Consider

First Community has an elevated exposure to commercial mortgages because of its loan portfolio allocation of 65%. Several articles have been published this year that predict an impending crisis for lenders caused by increased default rates, decreased property values, and liquidity constraints. The Mortgage Bankers Association noted an increase in delinquencies for commercial properties in Q1 of this year. Slowing economic growth in the second half of 2025 could cause further growth in this trend.

The large majority of the bank’s branches are located within the borders of South Carolina. First Community could be significantly impacted by any adverse political or economic events in that state. Key industries for South Carolina’s economy include aerospace, car manufacturing, and technology. All of these sectors are subject to possible future headwinds caused by tariffs and trade wars.

There are no guarantees that the merger with Signature Bank of Georgia will produce the expected results. The expansion puts First Community in a much larger metropolitan area that will be an incredibly competitive market. There could be conflicts between company cultures, challenges with staff integration, and unexpected complications in integrating the two companies’ IT systems. First Community may also have to combat leakage from Signature Bank customers who see the merger as an impetus to shop their accounts around to other local banks.

The Bottom Line

First Community is currently listed as a Buy by Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, and the stock has actually oscillated between Strong Buy and Buy for most of the last 12 months. The company is currently receiving a ratings boost from three increased revisions of FCCO’s earnings for FY 2025. The company shows no negative revisions.

While some of First Community's profitability metrics are just below the national averages for a bank of its size, there is enough positive here for me to rate the stock as a Buy. The company is expected to grow earnings by 38.5% in FY 2025 and another 15.9% in FY 2026. The upcoming merger also gives the bank access to the eighth-largest MSA in the US, providing new opportunities for growth and potentially making the bank more attractive to even larger banks that may want to take it over.

data.title: Meta Platforms Stock: Grown 700% Over The Last Decade, Still Room To Grow
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) delivered a blowout quarter as their Q2 revenue came in at $47.52 billion beating the consensus estimates by $2.68 billion while their EPS of $7.14 came in $1.24 ahead of what the street was looking for. META has become a printing press for profitability as their dominant position in the artificial intelligence (AI) race has fueled their ad revenue and user engagement across its platforms. I have been a long-term bull and believe META still has a long runway for growth despite climbing over 700% over the past decade. META has created an ecosystem that is unmatched and there is a large opportunity for AI agents to unlock opportunities in future sales while its wearable initiatives could unlock a tremendous amount of forward earnings as Mark Zuckerberg is adamant about owning the hardware that the next computing system runs on. For more than a decade META has had to cut Apple (AAPL) and Alphabet (GOOGL) in on the spoils from its apps since it didn’t own the Android or iOS operating systems. I think that the Ray-Ban smart glasses is just the beginning of an evolution outside of traditional screens and the investment community isn’t factoring in how much revenue can be generated from AI wearable devices in their future forms. I think that the earnings estimates at $6.65 for Q3 are low and META should generate another large beat which could take shares higher as they are currently trading at a low forward multiple. META is one of the companies I want to be in for the long-term and I believe shares will continue breaking out to the upside as time progresses.

Following up on my previous article about META

I previously wrote an article about META in the middle of May (can be read here) discussing why it was one of my favorite AI investments which I felt was undervalued. Since then shares of META have increased by 16.65% while the S&P 500 has increased by 9.68%. I had discussed how I believed META’s investments into AI would drive future growth in advertising, user engagement, and business messaging. There is a lot of opportunity for AI agent services to be sold to businesses across META’s platforms and tremendous amounts of untapped potential in it’s wearable unit. I am following up with a new article to discuss META’s Q2 earnings and why I still believe META can reach $800 by year end and exceed $1,000 in 2026.

Risks to investing in Meta Platforms

While I have been a shareholder of META since the IPO and I am still very bullish on their future growth opportunities, investors should do their own due diligence because the stock price may not perform how I believe it will in the future. META has continuously faced scrutiny from governments in many jurisdictions around data privacy , antitrust, and content moderation which could lead to future violations and fines worth billions. As privacy and data protection laws evolve META could find themselves non-compliant in several areas which could lead to lengthy litigation and legal fees. META has a treasure trove of data for hackers to feast on and is their security is breached it could lead to unauthorized access of client data or IP theft. This could cause new litigation due to their safeguards or cause customers to lose trust in META’s safety precautions causing their user counts to decline. There is also the risk that AI transforms how we interact with technology and social media starts to lose its appeal. Just because META has more than 1/3rd of the global population utilizing its products doesn’t mean that this moat will remain intact indefinitely. Investors should do their own research before allocating capital toward META.

Meta Platforms blew earnings estimates out of the water in Q2

META delivered what I consider to be astonishing numbers for a company its size for their Q2 earnings. META’s revenue increased YoY by 22% as they generated $47.52 billion as its family of apps accounted for $47.15 billion of its overall revenue. Reality Labs gained some traction as its revenue increased 5% YoY to $370 million due to its AI glasses. META still has a stranglehold on advertising as their advertising revenue increased 21% YoY to $46.56 billion as their customers experienced an 11% increase in ad impressions across its apps. META’s average price per ad increased by 9%, which indicated its customers were willing to pay up for advertising due to increased ad performance. META generated $20.44 billion in operating income which caused their operating margin to increase by 5% YoY from 38% to 43% in Q2. META’s net income increased 36% YoY to $18.34 billion which caused their diluted EPS to jump 38% YoY to $7.14. This allowed META to repurchase $9.76 billion worth of its class A shares while allocating $1.33 billion toward its dividend payments. When I look at META’s financials it’s hard to be bearish considering their generated $201.5 million per day in pure profit throughout the 2nd quarter. META is sitting on $47.07 billion in liquidity between its cash position and short-term investments with another $21.99 billion in long-term investments on the balance sheet. They can organically fund as much allocation toward CapEx as they feel is necessary and they are in a great position to shape how technology is utilized in the future.

Meta is aggressively positioning itself for future growth through its investments in AI as Mark Zuckerberg looks to unlock personal superintelligence which will empower individuals across the globe. META has established Meta Superintelligence Labs with a sole mission of accelerating META’s AI model development which includes Llama 4.1 and 4.2, and next-generation models. META is making the construction of compute infrastructure which includes the Prometheus cluster which is set to be the world's first gigawatt-plus cluster and Hyperion which is scalable to 5 gigawatts a cornerstone of their strategy. Mark Zuckerberg is convinced that AI will transform every aspect of business in the future and is sparing no costs through his aggressive investment strategy from hardware to top-tier talent, to make sure META is at the forefront of the next computing cycle. The problem that I see for anyone trying to compete with META is that they have more than 1/3rd of the globe using its apps with a war chest that only a handful of companies can match. META is so far in front that it could change how individuals utilize technology in the future.

META is focusing on several critical opportunities that can expand their top and bottom line through AI. The lion’s share of revenue comes from advertising and META is utilizing AI to enhance its ad system efficiency through AI-powered recommendation models which has led to approximately 5% more ad conversions on Instagram and 3% on Facebook. META’s generative AI features for ad creativity is helping smaller advertisers reach their audience and their Lattice model architecture is improving ad performance. META is focused on delivering more engaging experiences through AI-powered recommendation systems which have contributed to a 5% increase in time spent on Facebook and 6% for Instagram in Q2. META is delivering AI video editing tools and promoting original content while looking to integrate LLMs into Threads. META is focused on business messaging with agents and believes every business will incorporate this into their strategy as agents will be into ads and e-commerce platforms. Meta AI had over 1 billion monthly active users and META is seeing that there is increased engagement in WhatsApp for queries and content discovery on Facebook. META also is expanding its wearables from the Ray-Ban Meta glasses to Oakley Meta HSTN performance AI glasses which shows their dedication to integrating superintelligence into daily life. What I am most excited about is that META seems to be building out its infrastructure to potentially replace smartphones by offering multimodal AI interaction through an AI powered environment in their wearable glasses. I truly believe that if META marry WhatsApp and the rest of its environment into its glasses they could steal a huge amount of revenue from GOOGL and AAPL and become the largest company in the world.

The street isn’t bullish enough on Meta Platforms and I believe it’s going much higher

META has some of the best profitability metrics I have ever seen from a public company. From Q2 2023 to Q2 2025 META has increased its revenue by 48.49% ($15.52 billion) while it’s gross profit increased by 50.03% ($13.01 billion), operating income grew 113.46% ($10.87 billion) and its net income increased 135.45% ($10.55 billion). In Q2 2023 META generated $26.01 billion in gross profit for a margin of 81.29% while its operating income came in at $9.58 billion for an operating margin of 29.93% and its net income was $7.79 billion for a profit margin of 24.34%. In Q2 2024 META’s gross profit increased 22.11% ($5.75 billion) YoY while its operating income grew 55.04% ($5.27 billion) and its net income increased by 72.89% ($5.68 billion. META followed this up in Q2 2025 with an additional 22.86% ($7.26 billion) increase YoY in gross profit while operating income grew 37.68% ($5.59 billion) and its net income increased 36.18% ($4.87 billion). META is now operating at a 82.13% gross profit margin, an operating margin of 43.02% and a profit margin of 38.59%. These are incredible numbers and with the amount of capital being deployed to CapEx META can certainly keep this streak alive.

From an article I wrote on February 7th 2025 on Seeking Alpha (can be read here)

I don’t think the street is bullish enough about META’s profitability and what’s interesting is that the analyst community continues to increase their projections for META’s 2025 earnings but 2027 has remained relatively the same. The table below is from an article I wrote during Q4 earnings season when projections were being established for the 2025 fiscal year. META was projected to generate $25.24 of EPS in 2025 and $33.64 of EPS in 2027. META was expected to grow its EPS by 33.28% over the 2026 and 2027 fiscal years and traded for 28.21 times 2025 earnings and 21.16 times 2027 earnings. The analyst community has increased their projections for META’s 2025 earnings by 8.6% ($2.17) to $27.41 over the past 6-months but their projections for 2027 are basically the same at $33.71. This has made their forward growth over the next 2-years declined from 33.28% to 22.98% and META is still trading at 21.93 times 2027 earnings. I think that META’s forward earnings are being understated, and the analyst community should have increased their forward earnings by the same amount as the 2025 projections rather than leaving them the same. META trading at 21.93 times 2027 earnings looks very cheap and I think there will be a lot of surprises to the upside.

Conclusion

This was an incredible quarter for META that shattered the streets expectations. META delivered strong results and shares reached $796.25 several weeks after earnings. I think that the street and the investment community is underestimating how far ahead META is with their AI wearable division as they have an opportunity to transcend screens with how we interact with technology. META continues to become more profitable and over the past 6-months the street has increased their 2025 EPS estimates by 8.6% ($2.17) while keeping the 2027 estimates relatively flat. I think that META is priced for a breakout as their still trading at 21.93 times 2027 earnings with the ability to generate significantly more EPS in 2027 than the $33.71 that is expected. META’s forward growth in profitability while Reality Labs has lost between $4-$5 billion quarterly in operating income since Q2 2024 is impressive. I think the street is writing off Reality Labs as a massive failure but it could be the next large profit center for META over the next several years and if that occurs META could be dramatically undervalued.

data.title: Workday Post-Earnings: Classic Market Overreaction - Buy
data.description:
Earnings snapshot:

Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) stock is slipping in pre-market trading on Friday after the company’s 2Q26 report disappointed on guidance that came in line with expectations, despite a strong print altogether. The company reported EPS of $2.21 per share, above consensus at $2.11 per share, and revenue up 13% year over year at $2.35 billion, again above expectations at $2.34 billion. Net income was up to $228 million from $132 million in the year-ago quarter, and adjusted operating profit margin was also up to 29% from 24.8%, again beating consensus. According to the company’s CEO Carl Eschenbach, the results were backed by 1. AI, 2. Platform Innovation, and 3. International Momentum. He noted:

Customers are choosing Workday because we help them unlock value today and prepare for what’s next…Whether that’s navigating AI transformation, streamlining operations, or creating more meaningful work for their people.

The word “AI” was mentioned around 53 times during the earnings call, so management really tried to push that narrative; they are going all in on AI, and according to the CFO Zane C. Rowe:

[The company is] focused on making targeted and impactful investments to support long-term growth, such as expanding our AI talent, both organically and inorganically, entering new markets such as India, and investing in certain industries, including our federal business. At the same time, we are driving efficiencies across people, processes and systems, all accelerated by AI.

For the quarter, over 30% of the company’s customer base and 75% of their net new deals include one or more of their AI products, such as talent optimization, contract intelligence agent, recruiting agent, etc., making the net new ACV from AI products double year over year.

Management has been hyper-focused on developing a range of AI agents, mostly focused on human resources teams. Back in June, Workday released its latest AI agents, focused on extracting accounting details out of documents and also creating absent days lists. Just a week ago, the company acquired Flowise, and today, it announced another acquisition of AI startup Pradox Inc., with plans for both companies’ tools to be integrated into Wokrday’s software.

This is all good news, so why is the stock crashing on Friday?

Since the market is forward-looking, the company guidance rubbed investors the wrong way and set off alarm bells. The company expects $2.24 billion in subscription revenue for the current quarter and $180 million in professional services, for a total of $2.42 billion, in line with forecasts. Still, things aren’t looking too bad until we get to the adjusted operating margin guidance, at 28%, below estimates of 28.1%. The company, on the other hand, increased its FY26 forecast and is now expecting total revenue of $9.52 billion at the midpoint, reflecting 14.2% growth and also ahead of expectations at $9.4 billion.

The company is facing some pressures going forward, especially in the U.S. government and education sectors, due to reduced budgets. According to Eschenbach, “I think we’ll continue to see that as people are trying to figure out what the funding slowdown is going to look like, all the way to the state level.” President Donald Trump, back in March, signed an executive order to shut down the Department of Education. But that’s not all, as the market sells off this week due to fears of an AI bubble, especially after the DeepSeek moment a few months ago, investors are looking at the fast growth of AI industry, and how SaaS might be dead. Eschenbach dismissed these concerns and said that he thinks “his concern about AI disruption and the potential negative impacts for seat-based models are completely overblown…We hear that AI is eating the software world, and unless something’s changed from yesterday, AI is software, and we’re leaning heavily into it.”

Investment thesis:

I last wrote on Workday in late February, initiating the stock with a buy on the following. I am picking my coverage back up after my investment thesis, below, played out.

While the stock is down around 13.5% since my last buy rating, underperforming the S&P500, up 6.9% during the same period, I am maintaining the stock with a buy as I continue to believe the company’s fundamentals support further upside and the stock sell off post-earnings is an overreaction. Here’s a timeline of what happened since my coverage.

The stock is down around 12% year to date, also underperforming the S&P500, up around 8.4% during the same period. I think investors are best positioned to find an entry point at current levels.

Going forward, I am optimistic about the company’s aggressive push into AI through acquisitions. But it’s not all about AI. Workday is a mission-critical platform, “and platforms are sticky.” The company’s strength lies in its unified HR and finance platform, with structural growth coming from federal government adoption, as “government is leading deep into AI, they're looking to us to help them make this transition,” and international momentum, all backed by 97% retention, a $25B+ backlog, and improving margins (also, consistent with my expectations).

Valuation and price movement:

On valuation, the stock does still seem expensive at current levels, despite the pullback. Workday still trades at a premium to SaaS peers across most forward metrics, but relative to its own history, it has significantly de-rated. Going forward, the company needs flawless execution along with the premium valuation.

Forward Non-GAAP P/E of 25.96, slightly above the sector median of 23.01, but well below its 5-year average of 52.6x. While forward EV/Sales of 5.90x, nearly double the sector median of 3.04x, is still a discount to its own 5-year average of 8.7x. On Price/Sales, Workday is at 6.38x forward, over 2x the sector (3.1x), but again down significantly from its 9x+ historical range. Forward PEG remains attractive, currently at 1.1, below the sector at 1.8.

The pre-market drop of 4.3% puts the stock below the EMA21 at $227.6 and close to the recent support zone at $214 to $217. Instead of testing the resistance, the stock is sitting right above key support levels. EMA50 and EMA200 at $232.7 and $242.7 are now both acting as resistance if the stock tries to recover. RSI is at 48, but I anticipate it will drop as the market opens, as it’s already losing momentum. I recommend investors look at the $217 level, and if it holds at open, a small starter entry could be attractive, with a stop under the $214, but a safer entry would be if the stock stabilizes and reclaims the $225 on volume. If the support breaks below $214, the stock could pull back to the April low at $205, and I think that would be the next level to watch for an oversold bounce.

I hereon share my sentiment on Workday, and why I see more upside ahead in the second half of the year. Despite near-term volatility and cautious guidance, I see the company’s sticky platform, strong retention, and AI-driven expansion as key tailwinds that should unlock long-term potential for investors who are patient and willing to take advantage of the current pullback and support levels.

data.title: GigaCloud: The Market Still Doesn't See The Whole Picture
data.description:
My Thesis Update

GigaCloud Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:GCT) stock finally started to move to the side we, long-term GCT bulls, have been targeting for the past few months. While my very first call on GCT is still in the red, the most recent one saw GCT rise by almost 130%, besting the S&P 500 index (SP500) (SPY) by a factor of >9:

I think it's just the beginning of what seemed to be an inevitable outcome: The market quickly realized that it was wrong; GCT's Q2 FY2025 report, which came out in late July, proved that the whole business model is way more sustainable than some skeptics assumed and that the management's strategic plans are working indeed. I think the stock's repricing isn't done yet - GCT is simply too cheap, still. Until I see any major signs against my long-term optimism, I'm going to keep my "Buy" in active mode.

My Updated Reasoning

I couldn't have desired a better quarter from GCT and its executive team for Q2, which was actually the strongest quarter relative to what Wall Street analysts were expecting (i.e., in terms of beating), as GigaCloud managed to show a ~120% positive surprise to the bottom line with its $0.91 adjusted EPS figure:

It's interesting that the market waited for an EPS decline of over 35% YoY, but amid one of the strongest revenue beats in GCT's entire public history ($322 million vs. $290 million consensus, a beat of about 11.2%), the firm managed to show a positive EPS growth of 40% YoY.

GigaCloud's GMV surged by 31% Yoy and amounted to $1.4 billion, with the European end market showing a 59% YoY expansion for Q2 (all that amid a strict cost discipline). At a slightly lower GP and adj. EBITDA margins compared to Q2 FY2024 (the negative difference is ~1% in each of these), GCT managed to show a net profit margin of 11%, and it's a huge 200 bps improvement to last year's 9%:

I was telling my readers in my previous calls on GigaCloud that Europe is about to start offsetting some headwinds in the U.S. market. And it's now the case, as after opening the 6th fulfillment center in Germany during the quarter, Europe represents ~25% of their global revenue for the company, and it's still a huge untapped market for GigaCloud with a lot of expansion opportunities to offer in the next years.

The regional diversification is not the only thing that gives GCT an opportunity, though. Despite the bulls calling for the upcoming Noble House integration's benefits, the market completely missed it, so in Q2, when the management got "pleasantly surprised" by Noble's portfolio's contribution, the new updated portfolio is now "just 3 margin points behind legacy GigaCloud operations".

The strengthened SKU lineup, coupled with lean execution enabled by our marketplace has significantly improved the margin profile of the Noble House portfolio, now just 3 points behind legacy GigaCloud. We remain focused on disciplined execution. And by next summer, we expect the portfolio to settle into a more stable rhythm, shifting from a full overhaul to a balanced ongoing cadence of SKU refreshes and retirements as is typical for any healthy product portfolio.

Source: From GCT's Q2 earnings call.

Initially, GigaCloud was viewed by the market as a furniture reseller or furniture-specific B2B marketplace, but now the SKU categories look much more diverse, and it's unlikely to stay limited to just 50,000 SKUs if we try to envision the future.

At the same time, the active buyer base hitting 11,000 lately seems to be just the beginning because it's a pretty low base. It wouldn't be a low base if the SKUs weren't as diverse as they are now, but with the future expansion across new end markets, I believe that throughout calendar 2025-2027, the base can easily double in size.

I like that GCT stays a shareholder-friendly company in the sense of shareholders' returns. So far for 1H 2025, the firm generated ~$48 million in operating cash flow, and it allocated ~$46 million to buybacks, now holding ~$300 million in liquidity and 0 debt.

There are some headwinds on the horizon, and I should note them. For example, for the upcoming Q3, the management expects revenues at $302.5 million at the midpoint (-0.27% YoY effectively) with a GP margin contraction of ~2.5% because of tariff-related cost increases. I think it's a fair assessment, but it looks as if the management just tries to play it safe again and slightly underestimates the potential offsets coming from the European operations' success in building up. Be as it may, the annual revenue consensus suggests that GCT should make $1.20 billion for FY2025 and $1.26 billion for FY2026, and it still looks a bit pessimistic to me (the annual earnings revisions simply failed to reflect the Q2's strength, in my view):

The 40% YoY growth in EPS for Q2 wasn't reflected in the quarterly EPS revisions either:

I think the odds of Q3 beating, although it's just too early to say, are still quite high - the repricing of the stock took place after the Q2 print, but the estimates remained depressed, and they seem to incorrectly price in what's likely coming in the next few quarters.

Turning to the stock's valuation, I see that GCT's FWD P/E multiple expanded well in the past weeks, but it initially had a very low base back in spring 2025:

The current FWD P/E ratio of 9.6x looks absurdly low, considering the recent dynamics on the marginality levels and all those buybacks still taking place.

I suppose that GCT can beat the current FY2025 EPS by at least 10-15%, and if so, the implied figure would amount to $3.2625 instead of $2.90 (today's consensus). If we set a relatively low multiple of 12x earnings on that, the price target by the end of calendar 2025 will be 39.15/share, and it means that the upside potential would be in the range of 22-24%. So, the stock's recovery that began recently and the whole momentum strength should still be running hot.

Risks And Takeaways

There are some clear risks to my bullish update that I have to mention here. First, a lot depends on the macro situation. No matter how promising the newly-built business model might be, if consumer confidence and spending shrink next year or if the business activity stalls under the pressure of macro factors, GigaCloud's marketplace's growth will stall as well. Second, the management warned us about some headwinds for Q3, and while I think those will be temporary, the reality may go against my assumption. Third, the stock price action looks a bit overheated now, based on the weekly RSI indicator. I like to see GCT surge by almost 130% since my previous update, but it may be nearing a very strong resistance level, and it might be hard to get through that level if no multiple expansion comes.

Despite the above risks, I think that GCT is still a compelling "Buy" at its current price levels - the repricing isn't done yet because the earnings revisions that followed a phenomenal Q2 print simply failed to reflect the actual strength of the business model GCT managed to build. I believe GCT will try to get to the $40/share mark by the end of 2025, or likely earlier than that. So I keep my "Buy" in place.

Good luck with your investments!

data.title: Need Income? Beat Your Expenses With Dividend Growth Stocks
data.description:
Co-authored with Hidden Opportunities

Have you watched the movie Downsizing (2017) starring Matt Damon? This movie is based on the concept of people undergoing an irreversible procedure that shrinks them to just a few inches tall. Suddenly, their needs become tiny – miniature houses, miniature meals, miniature expenses. In this scaled-down world, their modest savings now afford them luxury and abundance far beyond what was previously possible.

In the real world, there’s no such procedure. Many retirees face the opposite – downsizing their lifestyles to fit within the limits of their savings. Inflation exacerbates the issue, quietly and relentlessly eroding purchasing power year after year.

But there’s another way. Instead of shrinking your lifestyle to match your savings, you can grow your income to match and even surpass your requirements. That’s where a comprehensive portfolio built upon the principles of diversification into income-producing investments and our Rule of 25 comes in.

Today, we’ll explore two such opportunities, both designed to help your savings grow without making you feel any smaller. Let’s dive in!

Pick #1: WES – Yield 9.6%

Western Midstream Partners LP (WES) is a midstream company that owns and operates over 14,300 miles of pipelines, including 8 natural gas and 11 crude oil / NGL pipeline networks, 21 gathering systems, and 77 processing and treating facilities. The company’s assets serve customers in the Delaware Basin (West Texas and New Mexico) and the DJ Basin (Northeastern Colorado). Additionally, the company maintains investments and joint ventures in assets located in South Texas, Utah, and Wyoming.

Note: WES is a Master Limited Partnership that issues a Schedule K-1 to investors

Recently, WES announced the acquisition of Aris Water Solutions in an equity- and cash-based transaction valued at approximately $1.5 billion. Aris holds assets, including 790 miles of produced-water pipeline, 1,800 MBbl/d of produced-water handling capacity, and 1,400 MBbl/d of water recycling capacity. The company operates in 625,000 dedicated acres through long-term, minimum-volume commitments with investment-grade counterparties. This transaction would essentially double WES’s capacity with produced water, and the company projects the acquisition to be accretive to 2026 FCF (Free Cash Flow), inclusive of estimated $40 million cost synergies.

Aris's assets extend WES's existing footprint far to the north into Lea and Eddy Counties, New Mexico, providing expanded access to the produced water handling needs in the Permian Basin. We believe this is a very lucrative position to be in, as the Basin is experiencing an increase in both gas and water production alongside its oil output. We also note that 72% of WES’s operating margin is the natural gas business, positioning the company well for the changing composition of America’s key energy belts.

The company also announced its second-quarter earnings on August 6th, reporting solid throughput increases across all product lines. Natural Gas throughput rose 3% sequentially, while Crude Oil and NGL throughput increased 6% sequentially. Produced water throughput experienced a 4% sequential increase.

WES reported $388.3 million in FCF during Q2, and distributed $355 million to unitholders, producing $33.1 million in FCF after distributions. The company ended the second quarter with a leverage ratio of 3x, and estimates 2025 throughput growth rates to be in mid-single digits for Produced Water and Natural Gas, and low-single digits for crude oil and NGLs. The midstream MLP reaffirmed its 2025 Adj. EBITDA range of $2.35 - $2.55 billion, Capex of $625 to $775 million, and FCF between $1.275 to $1.475 billion.

WES continues to punch above its weight, and presents a solid 9.6% yield from a rapidly expanding midstream company.

Pick #2: NNN – Yield 5.7%

NNN REIT, Inc. (NNN) reported its second-quarter earnings on August 5th, delivering solid financial and operational performance. At the end of the second quarter, NNN owned and operated 3,663 properties (up from 3,641 at the end of Q1), and reported an occupancy rate of 98%, with a weighted average lease term of 9.8 years.

The Triple-Net-Lease REIT reported a 1.2% YoY growth in Core FFO and AFFO per share to $0.84 and $0.85, respectively, reflecting continued growth in recurring cash flows. Management noted that during Q2, they leased seven properties to new tenants with rates at 105% of prior rents, reflecting strong demand for the REIT’s assets. Lease renewals are consistent with long-term trends of 85%, and new leases are on average 108% above prior rents. The Company achieved a 6.7% YoY increase in ABR (annualized base rent), demonstrating strong leasing momentum and the portfolio’s ability to combat inflation. A few weeks ago, NNN raised its quarterly dividend by 3.4% YoY to $0.60 per share, marking its 36th consecutive annual dividend increase.

NNN continued its robust investment activity, with $232.5 million deployed during the second quarter at an initial cap rate of 7.4%, and a weighted average lease term of 17.8 years. This brings the 1H 2025 total to $469.9 million, or 85% of the midpoint of the REIT’s full-year acquisition target. Additionally, NNN disposed of 23 properties for $51.2 million, at a weighted-average cap rate of 6.2%. A higher acquisition cap rate and a lower disposition cap rate naturally improve the overall income potential of the portfolio, indicating smart capital recycling by management. During the conference call, NNN raised its acquisition volume guidance by $100 million and its disposition outlook by $35 million to $120 million-$150 million for 2025.

NNN ended the second quarter with gross debt of $4.7 billion, with a weighted-average interest rate of 4.2% and a weighted-average debt maturity of 11.1 years. The company reported $939 million in total liquidity, including cash and an unused line of credit. We note that NNN issued a 4.60% 2031 senior unsecured note on July 1 to raise $500 million, which the company is using towards the repayment of the balance on its revolving line of credit. Pro forma for the 2031 notes brings the liquidity position to $1.4 billion.

Popular home goods chain, At Home, officially filed for bankruptcy in June, and plans to close 26 locations by September. NNN noted that they operate 11 of At Home’s locations, and none of them are included in the initial closure list. At Home remains current on all rent for these 11 locations following the filing.

The REIT raised its 2025 AFFO per share guidance to $3.40-$3.45, which places the recently raised dividend at a modest 70% payout ratio. This disciplined performance is enabled by NNN's strong balance sheet and high-quality portfolio, which continue to support its ability to raise capital efficiently (and inexpensively), pursue accretive acquisitions with confidence, and continue delivering generous dividend increases to shareholders.

Conclusion

Growing up, we fight countless battles – striving for the best education, the right career, and the opportunities that move us forward. In our working years, we overcome challenges to climb the ladder and secure our place.

In retirement, one of the most significant battles you will face is against an invisible enemy – inflation. Whether you notice it day by day or not, inflation steadily erodes your purchasing power, forcing many retirees to cut back. Your comfort in retirement depends on how immune you are to its effects.

At High Dividend Opportunities, our Income Method is built to help retirees win this battle – not by downsizing their lifestyle, but by growing their income. With the right investments producing reliable, rising cash flows, you can enjoy the retirement you worked so hard to earn, without making yourself any smaller. You can’t shrink your way to freedom. But you can grow your way there.

data.title: Marzetti Q4: Encouraging Retail Growth, Foodservice Remains Volatile
data.description:
The Marzetti Company (NASDAQ:MZTI), previously known as Lancaster Colony Corporation, reported the company’s fiscal Q4 results from the April-June period on the 21st of August. The consumer staples company showed improved growth in the retail channel, meanwhile the foodservice channel’s underlying growth was weak amid macroeconomic turbulence.

I maintained a Hold rating in my previous May article, titled “Lancaster Colony: Q3 Reflects The Impact Of Consumer Weakness”. The stock has since returned 11%, slightly below the S&P 500’s return of 14%.

Marzetti Q4: Marketing Investments Drive Solid Retail Growth, Foodservice Remains Volatile

Marzetti’s Q4 results came in slightly above expectations. Revenues came in at $475.4 million, up by 5.0% year-on-year and beating Wall Street’s consensus by a noteworthy $17.4 million margin. The adjusted EPS came in at $1.34, one cent above Wall Street’s consensus.

Notably, Marzetti returned to positive revenue growth – macroeconomic weakness previously caused the company’s revenues to decline in Q3 after a good long-term track record of growth, causing significant market concern. Even when excluding $12.2 million in non-core sales recorded during Q4, causing the majority of Marzetti’s topline beat, revenues grew at a fair 2.3% pace with stronger growth than in most previous quarters. The surface-level revenue strength was driven by both segments – retail revenues grew by 3.1% to $241.6 million and foodservice revenues grew at an especially good 7.0% rate to $233.9 million.

Retail sales continued to benefit from Marzetti’s licensing program and increased marketing investments, driving sales of Texas Roadhouse dinner rolls, Chick-fil-A sauces, Buffalo Wild Wing sauces, and other products as told in the Q4 earnings call. Marzetti’s own New York Bakery frozen garlic bread also drove growth in the retail channel among other branded products as the company noted strong market share gains with several brands, highlighting a strong overall retail performance. Grocery inflation stood at approximately 2.2% for the quarter, and Marzetti managed to grow slightly above inflation – Marzetti’s retail segment’s growth outlook is encouraging, albeit growth has partially been a result of increased marketing expenses.

The performance compares to many consumer staples peers’ weak sales growth, including Flowers Foods’ (FLO) organic -3.6% revenue decline, Nomad Foods’ (NOMD) -1.1% organic decline, and The Campbell Company’s (CPB) slow 1% organic growth.

More negatively, the foodservice segment’s strong growth was driven by non-core sales – excluding the impact of a temporary supply agreement, foodservice sales only grew by 1.4% year-on-year, reflecting a weak -1.7% volume decline but strong pricing power. In my opinion, the foodservice sales performance was quite bad; while macroeconomic weakness has continued to weigh on restaurant traffic as consumers remain cautious, National Restaurant Association’s survey data shows a noteworthy sequential improvement in traffic after a very weak early 2025. Foodservice remains Marzetti’s more volatile segment, experiencing pressure from swings in the economy despite serving consumer staples products.

Marzetti’s strong pricing power and cost savings programs attributed well towards profitability; the gross margin came in at 22.3%, up by 70 basis points year-on-year. The strong gross margin performance was offset by a $8.9 million increase in SG&A expenses, driven by marketing investments to fuel retail segment growth. Ultimately, while operating income even declined by -0.9% to $44.0 million when excluding restructuring charges, I believe that the quarter’s margin performance was encouraging. Increased marketing expenses should continue to drive retail segment growth in the future, gradually offsetting the impact of increased SG&A.

Marzetti’s verbal FY2026 outlook suggests a fair performance ahead. The retail segment is expected to continue benefiting from volume growth through licensing growth and select brands, and revenue from the foodservice segment is expected to benefit from Marzetti’s customer growth. At the same time, Marzetti does note the U.S. economic performance and consumer behavior as notable factors in the fiscal year’s performance, and I believe that especially the foodservice segment could see some volatility ahead.

Marzetti's Valuation Is Expensive

I updated my discounted cash flow (DCF) model for Marzetti. I assume nearly similar growth as previously at a FY2025-FY2035 CAGR of 2.8% and with 2.5% perpetual growth after the estimate period. Marzetti’s cost savings programs should continue to benefit margins, and I again assume the EBIT margin to reach a 13% level. Marzetti should continue to convert after-tax earnings relatively well into free cash flow.

The estimates put Marzetti’s fair value at $151.1, 15% below the stock price at the time of writing – even with fairly good earnings growth expectations, Marzetti’s stock seems to be moderately overvalued at a forward P/E of 25.7.

The fair value estimate is down slightly from $154.5 previously.

CAPM

The DCF model uses a cost of capital [WACC] of 6.89 %, derived from a capital asset pricing model:

I don’t estimate Marzetti to draw any debt. For cost of equity, I use the 10-year bond yield of 4.33% as the risk-free rate. The equity risk premium of 4.62% is Professor Aswath Damodaran’s most recent July estimate for the US. I have kept the beta estimate at 0.51. With a liquidity premium of 0.2%, the cost of equity and WACC both stand at 6.89%.

Takeaway

Marzetti’s total growth came in at a fairly encouraging level in Q2, benefiting from marketing investments in the retail channel but still reflecting turbulence in foodservice. Marketing investments weighed on profitability, but such pressure isn’t cause for concern as retail segment growth should gradually offset such margin pressure. Marzetti’s stock is quite expensive, not making the investment attractive at the current level. I remain with a Hold rating for MZTI.
